title: teucrium polium var. chamaedrifolium
intro: Teucrium polium var. chamaedrifolium, also known as the Woodland Germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium polium plant, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. This herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Woodland Germander include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. In herbal preparations, Woodland Germander is often used as a tincture or infusion, and can also be dried and used in teas, capsules, or as a topical cream to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
